bd-1.title: Test issue after reset
bd-1.description: Verifying the bd tracker was completely reset

bd-2.title: Blocking 5s network version check on startup
bd-2.description:
app_runner.py:353-365 always checks for updates unless NO_VERSION_UPDATE is set. version_checker.py:47 uses synchronous httpx.get(..., timeout=5.0). Can stall startup for 5 seconds. Also package name mismatch: pyproject.toml/init use 'codepp' but version_checker queries 'code-puppy'.

Fix: Make version checking async and cached. Cache latest version with 24h TTL. Show UI immediately, refresh in background. Fix package name to query actual published name.
comments:
**Summary**: Fixed blocking 5s network version check on startup

**Changes made**:
1. code_puppy/version_checker.py:
   - Added 24h TTL cache at STATE_DIR/version_cache.json
   - Added fetch_latest_version_async() with 2s timeout using httpx.AsyncClient
   - Added check_version_background() for fire-and-forget background refresh
   - default_version_mismatch_behavior() now checks cache only (instant, never blocks)
   - Fixed package name from code-puppy to codepp

2. code_puppy/app_runner.py:
   - Added asyncio.create_task(check_version_background(...)) after instant cache check

**Result**: Startup is now instant. Version check happens in background. Update notifications still work via cache or async refresh.

**Tests**: 13/13 passed in test_version_checker.py

bd-3.title: CLI bootstrap does eager work that blocks --help/--version
bd-3.description:
cli_runner.py immediately applies patches, imports config helpers, terminal_utils, and loads plugins BEFORE argument parsing. app_runner.py imports callbacks, config, console, keymap, version_checker at module level. terminal_utils.py has eager Rich import. This delays every invocation including --help, --version, shell completion.

Fix: Make main_entry() a tiny bootstrap that parses only enough to decide help/version, defer apply_all_patches(), load_plugin_callbacks(), and most app_runner imports to real execution path.
comments: **Resolution:** Made main_entry() a minimal bootstrap with fast path for --help/--version. Moved apply_all_patches(), plugin loading, and heavy imports to _run_full(). Made Rich import lazy in terminal_utils.py (behind TYPE_CHECKING). --help and --version now use only stdlib (importlib.metadata).

bd-4.title: Message queue has duplicate consumer threads causing race condition
bd-4.description:
MessageQueue._process_messages() (line 90-97) starts worker thread that notifies listeners. But SynchronousInteractiveRenderer.start() (renderers.py:189-195) adds itself as listener AND starts its own _consume_messages() thread that polls get_nowait() every 10ms. Renderer thread can steal messages before queue thread processes them, making wait_for_empty() inaccurate and _pending_count wrong.

Fix: Remove renderer's _consume_messages() thread, rely only on queue worker + listener callback.

bd-5.title: list_files does expensive per-file stat() on large repos
bd-5.description:
file_operations.py:343-349 processes up to 10,000 files. Line 362-418 does os.stat() for every file. Line 484-531 counts, sorts, materializes all items before building UI. Lot of syscall and allocation work for quick repo overview.

Fix: Add fast path for large recursive listings - skip per-file stats, skip total_size, avoid full materialization, only compute rich metadata for first visible subset or behind explicit 'detailed' mode.
comments:
**Resolution:**

Added FAST_MODE_THRESHOLD = 1000 to skip per-file stat() on large listings

- In fast mode: set size=-1 (unknown), skip stat syscalls entirely
- Updated FileEntry.size to accept -1 sentinel value
- Updated output to omit sizes when unknown
- Added "⚡ FAST MODE" indicator to output
- Summary only counts files with known sizes

Result: 10-100x faster for large repos (no stat on 10k files)

bd-6.title: Autosave rewrites entire session every turn - O(total history)
bd-6.description:
config.py autosave grabs full history on every save. session_storage.py:73-91 copies entire history list. session_storage.py:324-394 serializes whole payload and recomputes total tokens with sum(). Background thread avoids blocking but doesn't remove CPU/memory/disk cost. Gets expensive in long sessions.

Fix: Debounce autosave, skip if history length and tail hash unchanged, maintain incremental token total, consider append-only or chunked session storage.
comments:
**bd-6 - Autosave O(N) per turn:**
- Added debouncing (2s minimum between saves)
- Added fingerprint-based deduplication (history length + SHA256 of last message)
- should_skip_autosave() checks both conditions before any heavy work
- mark_autosave_complete() updates tracking state after successful save
- Skips unnecessary saves when history unchanged

Result: Long sessions no longer spam disk with redundant saves.

bd-7.title: Integrate EOL normalization overlay into Elixir FileOps
bd-7.description:
The eol.ex module exists in rewrite/code_puppy_elixir_rewrite_bundle/overlay/ but is orphaned and not in the production Elixir tree. This issue tracks integrating BOM stripping and CRLF→LF normalization into the main FileOps implementation.

**References:**
- Overlay file: rewrite/code_puppy_elixir_rewrite_bundle/overlay/elixir/code_puppy_control/lib/code_puppy_control/text/eol.ex
- Python source: code_puppy/utils/eol.py (normalize_eol, strip_bom, restore_bom, looks_textish)

**Acceptance criteria:**
1. Move/adapt eol.ex to elixir/code_puppy_control/lib/code_puppy_control/text/eol.ex
2. Add tests at elixir/code_puppy_control/test/code_puppy_control/text/eol_test.exs
3. Update FileOps.read_file to optionally apply EOL normalization
4. Ensure parity with Python heuristic (90% printable threshold, NUL detection)
5. Add integration test for BOM stripping on file read

**Priority reason:** FileOps parity blocker for Python→Elixir migration (code_puppy_python_to_elixir_plan.md PR 2)

bd-8.title: Port gitignore-aware filtering to Elixir FileOps
bd-8.description:
Python FileOps has gitignore-aware filtering via code_puppy/utils/gitignore.py (using pathspec library). Elixir FileOps currently walks directories without respecting .gitignore patterns, causing divergence in list_files and grep results between Python and Elixir backends.

**References:**
- Python source: code_puppy/utils/gitignore.py
- Elixir target: elixir/code_puppy_control/lib/code_puppy_control/file_ops.ex
- Usage: GitignoreMatcher.for_directory() caches compiled pathspec

**Acceptance criteria:**
1. Create CodePuppyControl.Gitignore module for pattern matching
2. Implement ancestor-walking to collect all applicable .gitignore files
3. Add pattern compilation caching (similar to Python LRU cache)
4. Integrate filtering into list_files and grep operations
5. Parity tests against Python fixtures with same .gitignore patterns

**Open questions:**
- Use nimble_parsec for pattern parsing or shell out to git check-ignore?
- Support .plandexignore/.cpignore later (out of scope for this issue)

**Priority reason:** Blocking FileOps parity - users see different file lists depending on backend (code_puppy_python_to_elixir_plan.md PR 3)

bd-9.title: Promote Repo Compass Elixir indexer from rewrite/ to production
bd-9.description:
The Repo Compass Elixir indexer was developed in rewrite/ folder (repo_compass.ex, indexer.ex, port.ex, repo_compass_test.exs) but never integrated into the production Elixir tree. The Python indexer in code_puppy/plugins/repo_compass/indexer.py remains the default.

**References:**
- Orphaned files: rewrite/repo_compass.ex, rewrite/indexer.ex, rewrite/port.ex, rewrite/repo_compass_test.exs
- Python entry: code_puppy/plugins/repo_compass/indexer.py
- NativeBackend route: code_puppy/native_backend.py::index_directory()
- Analysis: rewrite/ELIXIR_FIRST_REWRITE_ANALYSIS.md

**Acceptance criteria:**
1. Move rewrite/repo_compass.ex to elixir/code_puppy_control/lib/code_puppy_control/indexer/repo_compass.ex
2. Update rewrite/indexer.ex delegates and move to production
3. Ensure repo_compass_index/2 and repo_compass_index!/2 are exported
4. Route NativeBackend.index_directory() through Elixir when available
5. Verify parity: Python-style symbol summaries (class Greeter methods=hello,later, def wave(person, times))
6. Run integration tests and update port.ex routing if needed

**Dependencies:**
- Should follow bd-8 (gitignore support) for consistent file filtering

**Priority reason:** ELIXIR_FIRST_REWRITE_ANALYSIS.md identified this as highest-leverage first slice. Currently orphaned work.

bd-10.title: Implement standalone Elixir transport outside bridge mode
bd-10.description:
Currently Elixir functionality is only available through PythonWorker.Port (bridge mode) which requires Python ↔ Elixir integration. For full turbo_ops parity, Elixir file operations need to be accessible via standalone transport for non-Python consumers or direct usage.

**Current state:**
- Elixir control plane in elixir/code_puppy_control/
- Only entry point: CodePuppyControl.PythonWorker.Port.handle_file_request/3
- No standalone stdio service or socket interface

**References:**
- Current bridge: elixir/code_puppy_control/lib/code_puppy_control/python_worker/port.ex
- Target API: list_files, grep, read_file (equivalent to Python turbo_ops)
- fast_puppy_elixir_rewrite_plan.md Phase 2

**Acceptance criteria:**
1. Design transport protocol (stdio line JSON, Unix socket, or TCP)
2. Implement standalone service module in Elixir
3. Support request/response JSON-RPC style protocol
4. Maintain same security validations (sensitive path checks)
5. Python client adapter that mimics current FileOps API
6. Document when to use bridge vs standalone mode

**Open questions:**
- Keep Port for embedded use case, add service for standalone?
- Single-shot (CLI style) vs persistent daemon mode?

**Priority reason:** fast_puppy_elixir_rewrite_plan.md: Elixir becomes single native runtime surface, not just a bridge target

bd-11.title: Complete parse contract in NativeBackend (bd-64 follow-through)
bd-11.description:
NativeBackend has parse methods (parse_file, parse_source, parse_batch, parse_health_check, parse_stats) with bd-64 ghost reference in code, but the Elixir NIF routing may not be complete. The turbo_parse_nif.ex exists but integration status is unclear.

**Ghost reference:** native_backend.py line 64: 'bd-64: Phase 4 — Add Elixir NIF routing for parse operations'

**Current state:**
- turbo_parse_nif.ex exists: elixir/code_puppy_control/lib/code_puppy_control/turbo_parse_nif.ex
- NativeBackend.parse_* methods exist
- Phase 4 was described as 'Parse operations now route through NativeBackend with Elixir-first routing' in system prompt
- NativeBackend methods: parse_file (line 1130), parse_source (line 1189), parse_batch (line 1389)

**Verification needed:**
1. Does NativeBackend.parse_file actually route to Elixir NIF when elixir_first profile active?
2. Are parse_health_check and parse_stats implemented on Elixir side?
3. Does extract_syntax_diagnostics exist in NativeBackend?
4. Compare turbo_parse_bridge direct usage vs NativeBackend routing

**Acceptance criteria:**
1. Verify parse methods route to Elixir when available (elixir_first profile)
2. Add missing NativeBackend.extract_syntax_diagnostics wrapper
3. Ensure parse_stats/parse_health_check parity
4. Document which parse features use Elixir vs Rust vs Python
5. Remove ghost bd-64 reference or file follow-up issue

**Priority reason:** Phase 4 is claimed done in docs but verify actual implementation completeness.

bd-12.title: Consolidate migration planning documents into single source of truth
bd-12.description:
Repository has multiple migration planning documents that may contain drift or stale references:

**Existing docs:**
1. ELIXIR_MIGRATION_ROADMAP.md — Rust→Elixir analysis, tiered migration candidates
2. fast_puppy_elixir_rewrite_plan.md — Historical doc, still references removed Zig architecture
3. rewrite/ELIXIR_FIRST_REWRITE_ANALYSIS.md — Repo Compass indexer first slice justification
4. rewrite/code_puppy_elixir_rewrite_bundle/PLAN/code_puppy_python_to_elixir_plan.md — Practical PR sequence

**Identified problems:**
- fast_puppy_elixir_rewrite_plan.md: Zig references are historical/stale
- Multiple docs describe overlapping phases but with different numbering
- ELIXIR_FIRST_REWRITE_ANALYSIS.md says repo_compass is integrated, but code is in rewrite/ (orphaned)
- No single doc reflects current bd-7 through bd-12 work

**Acceptance criteria:**
1. Audit all four documents for conflicts and stale info
2. Update fast_puppy_elixir_rewrite_plan.md to remove Zig references (or mark historical)
3. Either:
   a) Consolidate into MIGRATION_STATUS.md with current state, OR
   b) Create clear hierarchy: ROADMAP (vision) + STATUS (current work) + PLAN (tactical)
4. Ensure all bd-* references in code match actual filed issues
5. Document which migration phases are DONE vs IN-PROGRESS vs TODO

**Priority reason:** Prevents confusion about what's actually implemented vs planned. Currently developers must check 4+ docs to understand state.

bd-13.title: Fix NativeBackend routing semantics - make it single Python entry point to native
bd-13.description:
NativeBackend routing currently has semantic drift between the named preference (RUST_FIRST) and actual behavior. This issue tracks fixing the backend to be the truthful source-of-truth for all native capability routing.

**Current Problems:**
1. **Misleading preference names**: RUST_FIRST profile silently prefers Elixir backends in practice - the naming doesn't match reality
2. **Direct native imports scattered**: Code outside of adapter paths imports directly from native bridges (turbo_parse_bridge, etc.) rather than going through NativeBackend
3. **Implicit capability routing**: It's unclear which backend (Rust crate vs Elixir service) handles file_ops, repo_index, parse, and message_core

**Required Fixes:**
1. Make backend preference semantics truthful:
   - RUST_FIRST should actually prefer Rust crates first
   - Add ELIXIR_FIRST for current Elixir-preferring behavior
   - Ensure profile naming matches actual routing behavior
   
2. Eliminate/track direct native imports:
   - Audit codebase for direct imports of turbo_parse_bridge, file_service_bridge, etc.
   - Route all native calls through NativeBackend adapter methods
   - Add linting or detection for direct bridge imports
   
3. Make capability routing explicit:
   - Document which backend handles each capability (file_ops, repo_index, parse, message_core)
   - Add runtime verification that NativeBackend routes to correct backend per capability
   - Ensure fallback chain (Elixir NIF → Rust → Python) is explicit and testable

**Migration Impact:**
This is a prerequisite for clean Phase 4 migration (bd-93, bd-64). Without truthful routing semantics, Pack Leader cannot accurately predict which backend will handle parse operations.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Profile semantics match names (RUST_FIRST = Rust first, not Elixir)
- [ ] Zero direct bridge imports outside NativeBackend adapter
- [ ] Capability routing table documented and verified at runtime
- [ ] Tests confirm correct backend selection per profile/capability
comments: Claimed by planning-agent-019d94 for implementation. Beginning work on NativeBackend routing semantics fixes.

Completed by planning-agent-019d95. All 4 acceptance criteria met:
1. Profile semantics match names (RUST_FIRST/ELIXIR_FIRST/PYTHON_ONLY) — done in prior work, verified
2. Zero direct bridge imports outside NativeBackend adapter — ~60 imports migrated, lint guard added
3. Capability routing table documented and verified at runtime — docs/acceleration.md + verify_routing()
4. Tests confirm correct backend selection per profile/capability — 145 tests pass (routing + fast_puppy + lint guard)

Changes pushed in commit 681e957e.
